51:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#embed)
52:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
53:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#embed)
54:     def embed(input, model: "text-embedding-3-small", **params)
55:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path("/embeddings"), headers)
56:       req.body = LLM.json.dump({input:, model:}.merge!(params))
57:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
58:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
59:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)
60:       res

64:     # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
65:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create OpenAI docs

67:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)

70:     # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
71:     #  When given an object a provider does not understand
72:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#complete)
73:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
74:       params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
75:       req, messages = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
76:       tracer.set_request_metadata(user_input: extract_user_input(messages, fallback: prompt))
77:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
78:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
79:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
80:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
81:       res
82:     end

178:     # @param query [String] The search query.

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Transport
4:   ##
5:   # Internal request execution methods for {LLM::Provider}.
6:   #
7:   # This module handles provider-side transport execution, response
8:   # parsing, streaming, and request body setup.
9:   #
10:   # @api private
11:   module Execution
12:     private
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # Executes a HTTP request
16:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Request] request
17:     #  The request to send
18:     # @param [Proc] b
19:     #  A block to yield the response to (optional)
20:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
21:     #  The response from the server
22:     # @raise [LLM::Error::Unauthorized]
23:     #  When authentication fails
24:     # @raise [LLM::Error::RateLimit]
25:     #  When the rate limit is exceeded
26:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
27:     #  When any other unsuccessful status code is returned
28:     # @raise [SystemCallError]
29:     #  When there is a network error at the operating system level
30:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
31:     def execute(request:, operation:, stream: nil, stream_parser: self.stream_parser, model: nil, inputs: nil, &b)
32:       stream &&= LLM::Object.from(streamer: stream, parser: stream_parser, decoder: stream_decoder)
33:       owner = transport.request_owner
34:       tracer = self.tracer
35:       span = tracer.on_request_start(operation:, model:, inputs:)
36:       res = transport.request(request, owner:, stream:, &b)
37:       res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
38:       [handle_response(res, tracer, span), span, tracer]
39:     rescue *transport.interrupt_errors
40:       raise LLM::Interrupt, "request interrupted" if transport.interrupted?(owner)
41:       raise
42:     end
43: 
44:     ##
45:     # Handles the response from a request
46:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
47:     #  The response to handle
48:     # @param [Object, nil] span
49:     #  The span
50:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
51:     def handle_response(res, tracer, span)
52:       res.ok? ? res.body = parse_response(res) : error_handler.new(tracer, span, res).raise_error!
53:       res
54:     end
55: 
56:     ##
57:     # Parse a HTTP response
58:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
59:     # @return [LLM::Object, String]
60:     def parse_response(res)
61:       case res["content-type"]
62:       when %r{\Aapplication/json\s*} then LLM::Object.from(LLM.json.load(res.body))
63:       else res.body
64:       end
65:     end
66:   end
67: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # {LLM::Context LLM::Context} is the stateful execution boundary in
6:   # llm.rb.
7:   #
8:   # It holds the evolving runtime state for an LLM workflow:
9:   # conversation history, tool calls and returns, schema and streaming
10:   # configuration, accumulated usage, and request ownership for
11:   # interruption.
12:   #
13:   # This is broader than prompt context alone. A context is the object
14:   # that lets one-off prompts, streaming turns, tool execution,
15:   # persistence, retries, and serialized long-lived workflows all run
16:   # through the same model.
17:   #
18:   # A context can drive the chat completions API that all providers
19:   # support or the Responses API on providers that expose it.
20:   #
21:   # @example
22:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
23:   #   require "llm"
24:   #
25:   #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
26:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
27:   #
28:   #   prompt = LLM::Prompt.new(llm) do
29:   #     system "Be concise and show your reasoning briefly."
30:   #     user "If a train goes 60 mph for 1.5 hours, how far does it travel?"
31:   #     user "Now double the speed for the same time."
32:   #   end
33:   #
34:   #   ctx.talk(prompt)
35:   #   ctx.messages.each { |m| puts "[#{m.role}] #{m.content}" }
36:   class Context
37:     require_relative "compactor"
38:     require_relative "context/serializer"
39:     require_relative "context/deserializer"
40:     include Serializer
41:     include Deserializer
42: 
43:     ZERO_USAGE = LLM::Object.from(
44:       input_tokens: 0,
45:       output_tokens: 0,
46:       reasoning_tokens: 0,
47:       input_audio_tokens: 0,
48:       output_audio_tokens: 0,
49:       input_image_tokens: 0,
50:       cache_read_tokens: 0,
51:       cache_write_tokens: 0,
52:       total_tokens: 0
53:     )
54:     private_constant :ZERO_USAGE
55: 
56:     ##
57:     # Returns the accumulated message history for this context
58:     # @return [LLM::Buffer<LLM::Message>]
59:     attr_reader :messages
60: 
61:     ##
62:     # Returns a provider
63:     # @return [LLM::Provider]
64:     attr_reader :llm
65: 
66:     ##
67:     # Returns the context mode
68:     # @return [Symbol]
69:     attr_reader :mode
70: 
71:     ##
72:     # @param [LLM::Provider] llm
73:     #  A provider
74:     # @param [Hash] params
75:     #  The parameters to maintain throughout the conversation.
76:     #  Any parameter the provider supports can be included and
77:     #  not only those listed here.
78:     # @option params [Symbol] :mode Defaults to :completions
79:     # @option params [String] :model Defaults to the provider's default model
80:     # @option params [Array<LLM::Function>, nil] :tools Defaults to nil
81:     # @option params [Array<String>, nil] :skills Defaults to nil
82:     def initialize(llm, params = {})
83:       @llm = llm
84:       @mode = params.delete(:mode) || :completions
85:       @compactor = params.delete(:compactor)
86:       @guard = params.delete(:guard)
87:       @transformer = params.delete(:transformer)
88:       tools = [*params.delete(:tools), *load_skills(params.delete(:skills))]
89:       @params = {model: llm.default_model, schema: nil}.compact.merge!(params)
90:       @params[:tools] = tools unless tools.empty?
91:       @messages = LLM::Buffer.new(llm)
92:     end
93: 
94:     ##
95:     # Returns the default params for this context
96:     # @return [Hash]
97:     def params
98:       @params.dup
99:     end
100: 
101:     ##
102:     # Returns a context compactor
103:     # This feature is inspired by the compaction approach developed by
104:     # General Intelligence Systems.
105:     # @return [LLM::Compactor]
106:     def compactor
107:       @compactor = LLM::Compactor.new(self, @compactor || {}) unless LLM::Compactor === @compactor
108:       @compactor
109:     end
110: 
111:     ##
112:     # Sets a context compactor or compactor config
113:     # @param [LLM::Compactor, Hash, nil] compactor
114:     # @return [LLM::Compactor, Hash, nil]
115:     def compactor=(compactor)
116:       @compactor = compactor
117:     end
118: 
119:     ##
120:     # Returns whether the context has been compacted and no later model
121:     # response has cleared that state.
122:     # @return [Boolean]
123:     # @api private
124:     attr_accessor :compacted
125:     alias_method :compacted?, :compacted
126: 
127:     ##
128:     # Returns a guard, if configured.
129:     #
130:     # Guards are context-level supervisors for agentic execution. A guard can
131:     # inspect the runtime state and decide whether pending tool work should be
132:     # blocked before the context keeps looping.
133:     #
134:     # The built-in implementation is {LLM::LoopGuard LLM::LoopGuard}, which
135:     # detects repeated tool-call patterns and turns them into in-band
136:     # {LLM::GuardError LLM::GuardError} tool returns.
137:     #
138:     # @return [#call, nil]
139:     def guard
140:       return if @guard.nil? || @guard == false
141:       @guard = LLM::LoopGuard.new if @guard == true
142:       @guard = LLM::LoopGuard.new(@guard) if Hash === @guard
143:       @guard
144:     end

146:     ##
147:     # Sets a guard or guard config.
148:     #
149:     # Guards must implement `call(ctx)` and return either `nil` or a warning
150:     # string. Returning a warning tells the context to block pending tool work
151:     # with guarded tool errors instead of continuing the loop.
152:     #
153:     # @param [#call, Hash, Boolean, nil] guard
154:     # @return [#call, Hash, Boolean, nil]
155:     def guard=(guard)
156:       @guard = guard
157:     end
158: 
159:     ##
160:     # Returns a transformer, if configured.
161:     #
162:     # Transformers can rewrite outgoing prompts and params before a request is
163:     # sent to the provider.
164:     #
165:     # @return [#call, nil]
166:     def transformer
167:       @transformer
168:     end
169: 
170:     ##
171:     # Sets a transformer.
172:     #
173:     # Transformers must implement `call(ctx, prompt, params)` and return a
174:     # two-element array of `[prompt, params]`.
175:     #
176:     # @param [#call, nil] transformer
177:     # @return [#call, nil]
178:     def transformer=(transformer)
179:       @transformer = transformer
180:     end
181: 
182:     # Interact with the context via the chat completions API.
183:     # This method immediately sends a request to the LLM and returns the response.
184:     #
185:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
186:     # @param params The params, including optional :role (defaults to :user), :stream, :tools, :schema etc.
187:     # @return [LLM::Response] Returns the LLM's response for this turn.
188:     # @example
189:     #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
190:     #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
191:     #   res = ctx.talk("Hello, what is your name?")
192:     #   puts res.messages[0].content
193:     def talk(prompt, params = {})
194:       @owner = @llm.request_owner
195:       compactor.compact!(prompt) if compactor.compact?(prompt)
196:       repair!(@messages, prompt)
197:       prompt, params, res = mode == :responses ? respond(prompt, params) : complete(prompt, params)
198:       self.compacted = false
199:       role = params[:role] || @llm.user_role
200:       role = @llm.tool_role if params[:role].nil? && [*prompt].grep(LLM::Function::Return).any?
201:       @messages.concat LLM::Prompt === prompt ? prompt.to_a : [LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
202:       @messages.concat [res.choices[-1]]
203:       res
204:     end
205: 
206:     ##
207:     # Ask a question and return the content string directly.
208:     # Accepts `with:` for file attachments and a block for streaming.
209:     # This interface is compatible with RubyLLM's `ask` method.
210:     # @param [String] prompt
211:     # @param [Hash] options
212:     # @option options [String, Array<String>, nil] :with
213:     #  File path(s) to attach
214:     # @option options [#<<, LLM::Stream, nil] :stream
215:     #  A stream target
216:     # @yield [String] content chunks when streaming
217:     # @return [LLM::Response]
218:     def ask(prompt, options = {}, &block)
219:       options = {with: nil, stream: nil}.merge!(options || {})
220:       with, stream = options.values_at(:with, :stream)
221:       prompt = with ? [prompt, [*with].map { local_file(_1) }] : prompt
222:       target = if block
223:         blk = block.dup
224:         blk.singleton_class.alias_method(:<<, :call)
225:         blk
226:       else
227:         stream
228:       end
229:       target ? talk(prompt, stream: target) : talk(prompt)
230:     end
231: 
232:     ##
233:     # @return [String]
234:     def inspect
235:       "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)} " \
236:       "@llm=#{@llm.class}, @mode=#{@mode.inspect}, @params=#{@params.inspect}, " \
237:       "@messages=#{@messages.inspect}>"
238:     end
239: 
240:     ##
241:     # Returns an array of functions that can be called
242:     # @return [Array<LLM::Function>]
243:     def functions
244:       return_ids = returns.map(&:id)
245:       @messages
246:         .select(&:assistant?)
247:         .flat_map do |msg|
248:           fns = msg.functions.select { _1.pending? && !return_ids.include?(_1.id) }
249:           fns.each do |fn|
250:             fn.tracer = tracer
251:             fn.model  = msg.model
252:           end
253:         end.extend(LLM::Function::Array)
254:     end
255: 
256:     ##
257:     # Returns whether there is pending tool work in this context.
258:     # This prefers queued streamed tool work when present, and otherwise
259:     # falls back to unresolved functions derived from the message history.
260:     # @return [Boolean]
261:     def functions?
262:       pending = queue
263:       (pending && !pending.empty?) || functions.any?
264:     end
265: 
266:     ##
267:     # Spawns a function through the context.
268:     #
269:     # When a guard is configured, this method can return an in-band guarded
270:     # tool error instead of spawning work.
271:     #
272:     # @param [LLM::Function] function
273:     # @param [Symbol] strategy
274:     # @return [LLM::Function::Return, LLM::Function::Task]
275:     def spawn(function, strategy)
276:       warning = guard&.call(self)
277:       return guarded_return_for(function, warning) if warning
278:       function.spawn(strategy)
279:     end
280: 
281:     ##
282:     # Returns tool returns accumulated in this context
283:     # @return [Array<LLM::Function::Return>]
284:     def returns
285:       @messages
286:         .select(&:tool_return?)
287:         .flat_map do |msg|
288:           LLM::Function::Return === msg.content ?
289:             [msg.content] :
290:             [*msg.content].grep(LLM::Function::Return)
291:         end
292:     end
293: 
294:     ##
295:     # Waits for queued tool work to finish.
296:     #
297:     # This prefers queued streamed tool work when the configured stream
298:     # exposes a non-empty queue. Otherwise it falls back to waiting on
299:     # the context's pending functions directly.
300:     #
301:     # @param [Symbol, Array<Symbol>] strategy
302:     #  If the stream queue already has tool work, `wait` will drain it
303:     #  without using this argument.
304:     #  Otherwise, this controls how pending functions are resolved directly.
305:     #  Use `:call` for sequential execution without spawning.
306:     # @param [Array<LLM::Function>] except
307:     #  A list of functions to exclude from the wait
308:     # @return [Array<LLM::Function::Return>]
309:     def wait(strategy, except: [])
310:       if LLM::Stream === stream && !stream.queue.empty?
311:         @queue = stream.queue
312:         @queue.wait
313:       else
314:         tools  = except.empty? ? functions : functions - except
315:         guards = guarded_returns(tools:)
316:         return guards if guards
317:         @queue = tools.spawn(strategy)
318:         returns = @queue.wait
319:         emit_tool_returns(tools, returns)
320:         returns
321:       end
322:     ensure
323:       @queue = nil
324:       @stream = nil
325:     end
326: 
327:     ##
328:     # Interrupt the active request, if any.
329:     # This is inspired by Go's context cancellation model.
330:     # @return [nil]
331:     def interrupt!
332:       pending = functions.to_a
333:       llm.interrupt!(@owner)
334:       queue&.interrupt!
335:       return if pending.empty?
336:       pending.each(&:interrupt!)
337:       returns = pending.map { _1.cancel(reason: "function call cancelled") }
338:       @messages << LLM::Message.new(@llm.tool_role, returns)
339:       nil
340:     end
341:     alias_method :cancel!, :interrupt!
342: 
343:     ##
344:     # Returns token usage accumulated in this context
345:     # @return [LLM::Object]
346:     def usage
347:       if usage = @messages.find(&:assistant?)&.usage
348:         LLM::Object.from(
349:           input_tokens: usage.input_tokens || 0,
350:           output_tokens: usage.output_tokens || 0,
351:           reasoning_tokens: usage.reasoning_tokens || 0,
352:           input_audio_tokens: usage.input_audio_tokens || 0,
353:           output_audio_tokens: usage.output_audio_tokens || 0,
354:           input_image_tokens: usage.input_image_tokens || 0,
355:           cache_read_tokens: usage.cache_read_tokens || 0,
356:           cache_write_tokens: usage.cache_write_tokens || 0,
357:           total_tokens: usage.total_tokens || 0
358:         )
359:       else
360:         ZERO_USAGE
361:       end
362:     end
363: 
364:     ##
365:     # Build a role-aware prompt for a single request.
366:     #
367:     # Prefer this method over {#build_prompt}. The older
368:     # method name is kept for backward compatibility.
369:     # @example
370:     #   prompt = ctx.prompt do
371:     #     system "Your task is to assist the user"
372:     #     user "Hello, can you assist me?"
373:     #   end
374:     #   ctx.talk(prompt)
375:     # @param [Proc] b
376:     #  A block that composes messages. If it takes one argument,
377:     #  it receives the prompt object. Otherwise it runs in prompt context.
378:     # @return [LLM::Prompt]
379:     def prompt(&b)
380:       LLM::Prompt.new(@llm, &b)
381:     end
382:     alias_method :build_prompt, :prompt
383: 
384:     ##
385:     # Recongize an object as a URL to an image
386:     # @param [String] url
387:     #  The URL
388:     # @return [LLM::Object]
389:     #  Returns a tagged object
390:     def image_url(url)
391:       LLM::Object.from(value: url, kind: :image_url)
392:     end
393: 
394:     ##
395:     # Recongize an object as a local file
396:     # @param [String] path
397:     #  The path
398:     # @return [LLM::Object]
399:     #  Returns a tagged object
400:     def local_file(path)
401:       LLM::Object.from(value: LLM.File(path), kind: :local_file)
402:     end
403: 
404:     ##
405:     # Reconginize an object as a remote file
406:     # @param [LLM::Response] res
407:     #  The response
408:     # @return [LLM::Object]
409:     #  Returns a tagged object
410:     def remote_file(res)
411:       LLM::Object.from(value: res, kind: :remote_file)
412:     end
413: 
414:     ##
415:     # @return [LLM::Tracer]
416:     #  Returns an LLM tracer
417:     def tracer
418:       @llm.tracer
419:     end
420: 
421:     ##
422:     # @return [LLM::Stream, #<<, nil]
423:     #  Returns a stream object, or nil
424:     def stream
425:       @stream || @params[:stream]
426:     end
427: 
428:     ##
429:     # Returns the model a Context is actively using
430:     # @return [String]
431:     def model
432:       messages.find(&:assistant?)&.model || @params[:model]
433:     end
434: 
435:     ##
436:     # @return [Hash]
437:     def to_h
438:       {
439:         schema_version: 1,
440:         model:,
441:         compacted:,
442:         messages: @messages.map { serialize_message(_1) }
443:       }
444:     end
445: 
446:     ##
447:     # @return [String]
448:     def to_json(...)
449:       LLM.json.dump(to_h, ...)
450:     end
451: 
452:     ##
453:     # Save the current context state
454:     # @example
455:     #  llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
456:     #  ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
457:     #  ctx.talk "Hello"
458:     #  ctx.save(path: "context.json")
459:     # @raise [SystemCallError]
460:     #  Might raise a number of SystemCallError subclasses
461:     # @return [void]
462:     def serialize(path:)
463:       ::File.binwrite path, LLM.json.dump(to_h)
464:     end
465:     alias_method :save, :serialize
466: 
467:     ##
468:     # @return [LLM::Cost]
469:     #  Returns an _approximate_ cost for a given context
470:     #  based on both the provider, and model
471:     def cost
472:       LLM::Cost.from(self)
473:     end
474: 
475:     ##
476:     # Returns the model's context window.
477:     # The context window is the maximum amount of input and output
478:     # tokens a model can consider in a single request.
479:     # @note
480:     #   This method returns 0 when the provider or
481:     #   model can't be found within {LLM::Registry}.
482:     # @return [Integer]
483:     def context_window
484:       LLM
485:         .registry_for(llm)
486:         .limit(model:)
487:         .context
488:     rescue LLM::NoSuchModelError, LLM::NoSuchRegistryError
489:       0
490:     end
491: 
492:     private
493: 
494:     ##
495:     # Binds runtime metadata onto an active stream.
496:     # @api private
497:     def bind!(stream, model, tools)
498:       return unless LLM::Stream === stream
499:       @stream = stream
500:       stream.extra[:ctx] = self
501:       stream.extra[:tracer] = tracer
502:       stream.extra[:model] = model
503:       stream.extra[:tools] = tools
504:     end
505: 
506:     ##
507:     # Returns the bound stream queue, if available.
508:     # @api private
509:     def queue
510:       [@queue, stream&.queue].compact.first
511:     rescue NoMethodError
512:       nil
513:     end
514: 
515:     ##
516:     # Loads skill directories and adapts them into tools.
517:     # @api private
518:     def load_skills(skills)
519:       [*skills].map { LLM::Skill.load(_1).to_tool(self) }
520:     end
521: 
522:     ##
523:     # Builds in-band guarded returns when the guard blocks tool work.
524:     # @api private
525:     def guarded_returns(tools:)
526:       warning = guard&.call(self)
527:       return unless warning
528:       tools.map { guarded_return_for(_1, warning) }
529:     end
530: 
531:     ##
532:     # Rewrites a prompt and params through the configured transformer.
533:     # @api private
534:     def transform(prompt, params)
535:       return [prompt, params] unless transformer
536:       stream = params[:stream]
537:       stream.on_transform(self, transformer) if LLM::Stream === stream
538:       transformer.call(self, prompt, params)
539:     ensure
540:       stream.on_transform_finish(self, transformer) if LLM::Stream === stream
541:     end
542: 
543:     ##
544:     # Executes a turn through the Responses API.
545:     # @api private
546:     def respond(prompt, params)
547:       params = @params.merge(params)
548:       prompt, params = transform(prompt, params)
549:       bind!(params[:stream], params[:model], params[:tools])
550:       res_id = params[:store] == false ? nil : @messages.find(&:assistant?)&.response&.response_id
551:       params = params.merge(previous_response_id: res_id, input: @messages.to_a).compact
552:       [prompt, params, @llm.responses.create(prompt, params)]
553:     end
554: 
555:     ##
556:     # Executes a turn through the chat completions API.
557:     # @api private
558:     def complete(prompt, params)
559:       params = params.merge(messages: @messages.to_a)
560:       params = @params.merge(params)
561:       prompt, params = transform(prompt, params)
562:       bind!(params[:stream], params[:model], params[:tools])
563:       [prompt, params, @llm.complete(prompt, params)]
564:     end
565: 
566:     ##
567:     # Builds one guarded tool return for a blocked function call.
568:     # @api private
569:     def guarded_return_for(function, warning)
570:       LLM::Function::Return.new(function.id, function.name, {
571:         error: true,
572:         type: LLM::GuardError.name,
573:         message: warning
574:       })
575:     end
576: 
577:     ##
578:     # Emits tool return callbacks for directly waited function work.
579:     # @api private
580:     def emit_tool_returns(tools, returns)
581:       return unless LLM::Stream === stream
582:       returns.each_with_index { |result, index| stream.on_tool_return(tools[index], result) }
583:     end
584: 
585:     ##
586:     # Closes assistant tool-call messages that do not have matching tool
587:     # responses. This can happen when a turn is interrupted while a tool
588:     # call is streaming or waiting for user confirmation.
589:     # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
590:     # @param [Object] prompt
591:     # @return [void]
592:     def repair!(messages, prompt)
593:       message = messages.last
594:       return unless message&.tool_call?
595:       returns = self.returns + [*prompt].grep(LLM::Function::Return)
596:       cancelled = []
597:       [*message.extra.tool_calls].each do |tool|
598:         next if returns.any? { _1.id == tool[:id] }
599:         attrs = {cancelled: true, reason: "function call cancelled"}
600:         cancelled << LLM::Function::Return.new(tool.id, tool.name, attrs)
601:       end
602:       messages << LLM::Message.new(@llm.tool_role, cancelled) unless cancelled.empty?
603:     end
604:   end
605: end

3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # {LLM::Response LLM::Response} is the normalized base shape for
6:   # provider and endpoint responses in llm.rb.
7:   #
8:   # Provider calls return an instance of this class, then extend it
9:   # with provider-, endpoint-, or context-specific modules so response
10:   # handling can share one common surface without flattening away
11:   # specialized behavior.
12:   #
13:   # The normalized response keeps the transport response available
14:   # through {#res}. When the default net/http transport is in use,
15:   # {LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP
16:   # LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP} keeps the
17:   # original {Net::HTTPResponse Net::HTTPResponse} available through
18:   # its own {LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP#res #res}.
19:   class Response
20:     require "json"
21: 
22:     ##
23:     # Returns the HTTP response
24:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
25:     attr_reader :res
26: 
27:     ##
28:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
29:     #  HTTP response
30:     # @return [LLM::Response]
31:     #  Returns an instance of LLM::Response
32:     def initialize(res)
33:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
34:     end
35: 
36:     ##
37:     # Returns the response body
38:     # @return [LLM::Object, String]
39:     #  Returns an LLM::Object when the response body is JSON,
40:     #  otherwise returns a raw string.
41:     def body
42:       @res.body
43:     end
44: 
45:     ##
46:     # Returns an inspection of the response object
47:     # @return [String]
48:     def inspect
49:       "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)} @body=#{body.inspect} @res=#{@res.inspect}>"
50:     end
51: 
52:     ##
53:     # Returns true if the response is successful
54:     # @return [Boolean]
55:     def ok?
56:       @res.success?
57:     end
58: 
59:     ##
60:     # Returns true if the response is from the Files API
61:     # @return [Boolean]
62:     def file?
63:       false
64:     end
65: 
66:     private
67: 
68:     def method_missing(m, *args, **kwargs, &b)
69:       if LLM::Object === body
70:         body.respond_to?(m) ? body[m.to_s] : super
71:       else
72:         super
73:       end
74:     end
75: 
76:     def respond_to_missing?(m, include_private = false)
77:       if LLM::Object === body
78:         body.respond_to?(m)
79:       else
80:         false
81:       end
82:     end
83:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Ollama class implements a provider for [Ollama](https://ollama.ai/) –
6:   # and the provider supports a wide range of models. It is straight forward
7:   # to run on your own hardware, and there are a number of multi-modal models
8:   # that can process both images and text.
9:   #
10:   # @example
11:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
12:   #   require "llm"
13:   #
14:   #   llm = LLM.ollama(key: nil)
15:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm, model: "llava")
16:   #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this image", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
17:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
18:   class Ollama < Provider
19:     require_relative "ollama/error_handler"
20:     require_relative "ollama/request_adapter"
21:     require_relative "ollama/response_adapter"
22:     require_relative "ollama/stream_parser"
23:     require_relative "ollama/models"
24: 
25:     include RequestAdapter
26: 
27:     HOST = "localhost"
28: 
29:     ##
30:     # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
31:     def initialize(**)
32:       super(host: HOST, port: 11434, ssl: false, **)
33:     end
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @return [Symbol]
37:     #  Returns the provider's name
38:     def name
39:       :ollama
40:     end
41: 
42:     ##
43:     # Provides an embedding
44:     # @param input (see LLM::Provider#embed)
45:     # @param model (see LLM::Provider#embed)
46:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#embed)
47:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
48:     # @return [LLM::Response]
49:     def embed(input, model: default_model, **params)
50:       params   = {model:}.merge!(params)
51:       req      = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/v1/embeddings", headers)
52:       req.body = LLM.json.dump({input:}.merge!(params))
53:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
54:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
55:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)
56:       res
57:     end
58: 
59:     ##
60:     # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
61:     # @see https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md#generate-a-chat-completion Ollama docs
62:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
63:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
64:     # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
65:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
66:     # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
67:     #  When given an object a provider does not understand
68:     # @return [LLM::Response]
69:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
70:       params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
71:       req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
72:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
73:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
74:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
75:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
76:       res
77:     end
78: 
79:     ##
80:     # Provides an interface to Ollama's models API
81:     # @see https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md#list-local-models Ollama docs
82:     # @return [LLM::Ollama::Models]
83:     def models
84:       LLM::Ollama::Models.new(self)
85:     end
86: 
87:     ##
88:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#assistant_role)
89:     def assistant_role
90:       "assistant"
91:     end
92: 
93:     ##
94:     # Returns the default model for chat completions
95:     # @see https://ollama.com/library/qwen3 qwen3
96:     # @return [String]
97:     def default_model
98:       "qwen3:latest"
99:     end
100: 
101:     private
102: 
103:     def headers
104:       lock do
105:         (@headers || {}).merge(
106:           "Content-Type" => "application/json",
107:           "Authorization" => "Bearer #{@key}"
108:         )
109:       end
110:     end
111: 
112:     def stream_parser
113:       LLM::Ollama::StreamParser
114:     end
115: 
116:     def error_handler
117:       LLM::Ollama::ErrorHandler
118:     end
119: 
120:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
121:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model, stream: true}.merge!(params)
122:       tools  = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
123:       params = [params, {format: params[:schema]}, adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
124:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
125:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
126:       [params, stream, tools, role]
127:     end
128: 
129:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
130:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
131:       body = LLM.json.dump({messages: [adapt(messages)].flatten}.merge!(params))
132:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/api/chat", headers)
133:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
134:       req
135:     end
136: 
137:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
138:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
139:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt.to_a]
140:       else
141:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
142:       end
143:     end
144:   end
145: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Bedrock class implements a provider for
6:   # [Amazon Bedrock](https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/).
7:   #
8:   # Bedrock provides access to foundation models from Anthropic, Meta,
9:   # Mistral, AI21 Labs, Cohere, and more through the AWS infrastructure.
10:   # This provider uses the Bedrock Converse API for chat completions,
11:   # and the Converse Stream API for streaming.
12:   #
13:   # Unlike other llm.rb providers which use API key authentication,
14:   # Bedrock uses AWS Signature V4 (SigV4) for request signing.
15:   # You must provide AWS credentials (access key, secret key, and region)
16:   # instead of a single API key.
17:   #
18:   # Streaming uses the AWS Event Stream binary protocol instead of
19:   # standard SSE. The binary framing is decoded inline using only
20:   # Ruby's stdlib.
21:   #
22:   # @example
23:   #   require "llm"
24:   #
25:   #   llm = LLM.bedrock(
26:   #     access_key_id: ENV["AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"],
27:   #     secret_access_key: ENV["AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"],
28:   #     region: "us-east-1"
29:   #   )
30:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
31:   #   ctx.talk "Hello, how are you?"
32:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { puts _1.content }
33:   class Bedrock < Provider
34:     require_relative "bedrock/signature"
35:     require_relative "bedrock/error_handler"
36:     require_relative "bedrock/request_adapter"
37:     require_relative "bedrock/request_adapter/completion"
38:     require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter"
39:     require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter/completion"
40:     require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter/models"
41:     require_relative "bedrock/stream_decoder"
42:     require_relative "bedrock/stream_parser"
43:     require_relative "bedrock/models"
44: 
45:     include RequestAdapter
46: 
47:     HOST_PATTERN = "bedrock-runtime.%s.amazonaws.com"
48: 
49:     ##
50:     # @param [String] access_key_id AWS access key ID
51:     # @param [String] secret_access_key AWS secret access key
52:     # @param [String] region AWS region (e.g. "us-east-1")
53:     # @param [String, nil] session_token AWS session token for temporary credentials
54:     # @param [String, nil] host Override the Bedrock API host
55:     # @param [Integer] port Connection port
56:     # @param [Boolean] ssl Whether to use SSL
57:     # @param [Integer] timeout Request timeout in seconds
58:     def initialize(access_key_id: nil, secret_access_key: nil,
59:                    region: nil, session_token: nil,
60:                    host: nil, port: 443, ssl: true, timeout: 60,
61:                    **)
62:       region ||= "us-east-1"
63:       @access_key_id = access_key_id
64:       @secret_access_key = secret_access_key
65:       @aws_region = region
66:       @session_token = session_token
67:       host ||= HOST_PATTERN % region
68:       @aws_host = host
69:       super(key: @access_key_id, host:, port:, ssl:, timeout:, persistent: false)
70:     end
71: 
72:     ##
73:     # @return [Symbol] Returns the provider's name
74:     def name
75:       :bedrock
76:     end
77: 
78:     ##
79:     # Provides an interface to the Bedrock Converse API
80:     #
81:     # @see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/APIReference/API_runtime_Converse.html
82:     #
83:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
84:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
85:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#complete)
86:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
87:       params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
88:       req, messages, body = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, stream:)
89:       tracer.set_request_metadata(user_input: extract_user_input(messages, fallback: prompt))
90:       sign!(req, body)
91:       model_id = model_id_for(req.path)
92:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream:, operation: "chat", stream_parser:, model: model_id)
93:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
94:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
95:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: model_id, res:, span:)
96:       res
97:     end
98: 
99:     ##
100:     # Provides an interface to Bedrock's ListFoundationModels API.
101:     #
102:     # @note
103:     #  Unlike the Converse API (bedrock-runtime), this endpoint lives

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM::OpenAI::RequestAdapter
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class Completion
7:     ##
8:     # @param [LLM::Message, Hash] message
9:     #  The message to format
10:     def initialize(message)
11:       @message = message
12:     end
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # Adapts the message for the OpenAI chat completions API
16:     # @return [Hash]
17:     def adapt
18:       catch(:abort) do
19:         if Hash === message
20:           {role: message[:role], content: adapt_content(message[:content])}
21:         elsif message.tool_call?
22:           {role: message.role, content: nil, tool_calls: message.extra[:original_tool_calls]}
23:         else
24:           adapt_message
25:         end
26:       end
27:     end
28: 
29:     private
30: 
31:     def adapt_message
32:       case content
33:       when Array
34:         adapt_array
35:       else
36:         {role: message.role, content: adapt_content(content)}
37:       end
38:     end
39: 
40:     def adapt_array
41:       if content.empty?
42:         nil
43:       elsif returns.any?
44:         returns.map { {role: "tool", tool_call_id: _1.id, content: LLM.json.dump(_1.value)} }
45:       else
46:         {role: message.role, content: content.flat_map { adapt_content(_1) }}
47:       end
48:     end
49: 
50:     def adapt_content(content)
51:       case content
52:       when LLM::Object
53:         adapt_object(content)
54:       when String
55:         [{type: :text, text: content.to_s}]
56:       when LLM::Response
57:         adapt_remote_file(content)
58:       when LLM::Message
59:         adapt_content(content.content)
60:       when LLM::Function::Return
61:         throw(:abort, {role: "tool", tool_call_id: content.id, content: LLM.json.dump(content.value)})
62:       else
63:         prompt_error!(content)
64:       end
65:     end
66: 
67:     def adapt_object(object)
68:       case object.kind
69:       when :image_url
70:         [{type: :image_url, image_url: {url: object.value}}]
71:       when :local_file
72:         adapt_local_file(object.value)
73:       when :remote_file
74:         adapt_remote_file(object.value)
75:       else
76:         prompt_error!(object)
77:       end
78:     end
79: 
80:     def adapt_local_file(file)
81:       if file.image?
82:         [{type: :image_url, image_url: {url: file.to_data_uri}}]
83:       else
84:         [{type: :file, file: {filename: file.basename, file_data: file.to_data_uri}}]
85:       end
86:     end
87: 
88:     def adapt_remote_file(file)
89:       if file.file?
90:         [{type: :file, file: {file_id: file.id}}]
91:       else
92:         prompt_error!(file)
93:       end
94:     end
95: 
96:     def prompt_error!(content)
97:       if LLM::Object === content
98:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given LLM::Object with kind '#{content.kind}' is not " \
99:                                 "supported by the OpenAI chat completions API."
100:       else
101:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{content.class}) " \
102:                                 "is not supported by the OpenAI chat completions API."
103:       end
104:     end
105: 
106:     def message = @message
107:     def content = message.content
108:     def returns = content.grep(LLM::Function::Return)
109:   end
110: end

3: class LLM::Anthropic
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   module RequestAdapter
7:     require_relative "request_adapter/completion"
8: 
9:     ##
10:     # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
11:     #  The messages to adapt
12:     # @return [Hash]
13:     def adapt(messages, mode: nil)
14:       payload = {messages: [], system: []}
15:       messages.each do |message|
16:         adapted = Completion.new(message).adapt
17:         next if adapted.nil?
18:         if system?(message)
19:           payload[:system].concat Array(adapted[:content])
20:         else
21:           payload[:messages] << adapted
22:         end
23:       end
24:       payload.delete(:system) if payload[:system].empty?
25:       payload
26:     end
27:

29:

31:     # @param [Hash] params
32:     # @return [Hash]
33:     def adapt_tools(tools)
34:       return {} unless tools&.any?
35:       {tools: tools.map { _1.respond_to?(:adapt) ? _1.adapt(self) : _1 }}
36:     end

38:     def system?(message)
39:       if message.respond_to?(:system?)
40:         message.system?
41:       else
42:         Hash === message and message[:role].to_s == "system"

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::OpenAI
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class ErrorHandler
7:     ##
8:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
9:     #  Non-2XX response from the server
10:     attr_reader :res
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @return [Object, nil]
14:     #  The span
15:     attr_reader :span
16: 
17:     ##
18:     # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
19:     #  The tracer
20:     # @param [Object, nil] span
21:     #  The span
22:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
23:     #  The response from the server
24:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::ErrorHandler]
25:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)
26:       @tracer = tracer
27:       @span = span
28:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
33:     #  Raises a subclass of {LLM::Error LLM::Error}
34:     def raise_error!
35:       ex = error
36:       @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
37:     ensure
38:       raise(ex) if ex
39:     end
40: 
41:     private
42: 
43:     ##
44:     # @return [String, LLM::Object]
45:     def body
46:       @body ||= parse_body!
47:     end
48: 
49:     ##
50:     # @return [LLM::Error]
51:     def error
52:       if res.server_error?
53:         LLM::ServerError.new("Server error").tap { _1.response = res }
54:       elsif res.unauthorized?
55:         LLM::UnauthorizedError.new("Authentication error").tap { _1.response = res }
56:       elsif res.rate_limited?
57:         LLM::RateLimitError.new("Too many requests").tap { _1.response = res }
58:       elsif res.not_found?
59:         LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
60:       else
61:         error = body["error"] || {}
62:         case error["type"]
63:         when "invalid_request_error" then handle_invalid_request(error)
64:         when "server_error"
65:           LLM::ServerError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
66:         else
67:           LLM::Error.new(error["message"] || "Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
68:         end
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     ##
73:     # @param [Exception] error
74:     # @return [LLM::Error]
75:     def handle_invalid_request(error)
76:       case error["code"]
77:       when "context_length_exceeded"
78:         LLM::ContextWindowError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
79:       else
80:         LLM::InvalidRequestError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
81:       end
82:     end
83: 
84:     ##
85:     # Tries to parse the response body as a LLM::Object
86:     # @return [String, LLM::Object]
87:     def parse_body!
88:       if String === res.body
89:         LLM::Object.from LLM.json.load(res.body)
90:       elsif Hash === res.body
91:         LLM::Object.from(res.body)
92:       else
93:         res.body
94:       end
95:     rescue
96:       res.body
97:     end
98:   end
99: end

2: 
3: class LLM::Bedrock
4:   ##
5:   # Handles Bedrock API error responses.
6:   #
7:   # Bedrock errors come as JSON with:
8:   #   { "message" => "...", "__type" => "..." }
9:   # or as standard HTTP status codes.
10:   #
11:   # @api private
12:   class ErrorHandler
13:     ##
14:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
15:     attr_reader :res
16: 
17:     ##
18:     # @return [Object, nil]
19:     attr_reader :span
20: 
21:     ##
22:     # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
23:     # @param [Object, nil] span
24:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
25:     # @return [LLM::Bedrock::ErrorHandler]
26:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)
27:       @tracer = tracer
28:       @span = span
29:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
30:     end
31: 
32:     ##
33:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
34:     def raise_error!
35:       ex = error
36:       @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
37:     ensure
38:       raise(ex) if ex
39:     end
40: 
41:     private
42: 
43:     ##
44:     # @return [LLM::Error]
45:     def error
46:       message = extract_message
47:       if res.server_error?
48:         LLM::ServerError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
49:       elsif res.unauthorized?
50:         LLM::UnauthorizedError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
51:       elsif res.forbidden?
52:         LLM::UnauthorizedError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
53:       elsif res.rate_limited?
54:         LLM::RateLimitError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
55:       elsif res.not_found?
56:         LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
57:       else
58:         LLM::Error.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
59:       end
60:     end
61: 
62:     ##
63:     # @return [String]
64:     def extract_message
65:       body = parse_body
66:       body["message"] || body["Message"] || body["__type"] || "Unexpected error"
67:     end
68: 
69:     ##
70:     # @return [Hash]
71:     def parse_body
72:       return {} if res.body.nil? || res.body.empty?
73:       parsed = LLM.json.load(res.body.dup.force_encoding(Encoding::UTF_8).scrub)
74:       Hash === parsed ? parsed : {}
75:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
76:       {}
77:     end
78:   end
79: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Anthropic
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class StreamParser
7:     ##
8:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
9:     # @return [Hash]
10:     attr_reader :body
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @param [#<<, LLM::Stream] stream
14:     #  A stream sink that implements {#<<} or the {LLM::Stream} interface
15:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::StreamParser]
16:     def initialize(stream)
17:       @body = {"role" => "assistant", "content" => []}
18:       @stream = stream
19:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
20:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
21:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
22:     end
23: 
24:     ##
25:     # @param [Hash] chunk
26:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::StreamParser]
27:     def parse!(chunk)
28:       tap { merge!(chunk) }
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
33:     # @return [void]
34:     def free
35:     end
36: 
37:     private
38: 
39:     def merge!(chunk)
40:       if chunk["type"] == "message_start"
41:         merge_message!(chunk["message"])
42:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_start"
43:         @body["content"][chunk["index"]] = chunk["content_block"]
44:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_delta"
45:         if chunk["delta"]["type"] == "text_delta"
46:           @body["content"][chunk["index"]]["text"] << chunk["delta"]["text"]
47:           emit_content(chunk["delta"]["text"])
48:         elsif chunk["delta"]["type"] == "input_json_delta"
49:           content = @body["content"][chunk["index"]]
50:           if Hash === content["input"]
51:             content["input"] = chunk["delta"]["partial_json"]
52:           else
53:             content["input"] ||= +""
54:             content["input"] << chunk["delta"]["partial_json"]
55:           end
56:         end
57:       elsif chunk["type"] == "message_delta"
58:         merge_message!(chunk["delta"]) if chunk["delta"]
59:         extras = chunk.reject { |k, _| k == "type" || k == "delta" }
60:         merge_message!(extras) unless extras.empty?
61:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_stop"
62:         content = @body["content"][chunk["index"]]
63:         if content["input"]
64:           content["input"] = LLM.json.load(content["input"])
65:         end
66:         if content["type"] == "tool_use"
67:           emit_tool(content)
68:         end
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     def merge_message!(message)
73:       message.each_pair do |key, value|
74:         if value.respond_to?(:each_pair)
75:           @body[key] ||= {}
76:           deep_merge!(@body[key], value)
77:         else
78:           @body[key] = value
79:         end
80:       end
81:     end
82: 
83:     def deep_merge!(target, source)
84:       source.each_pair do |key, value|
85:         if value.respond_to?(:each_pair) && target[key].respond_to?(:each_pair)
86:           deep_merge!(target[key], value)
87:         else
88:           target[key] = value
89:         end
90:       end
91:     end
92: 
93:     def emit_content(value)
94:       if @can_emit_content
95:         @stream.on_content(value)
96:       elsif @can_push_content
97:         @stream << value
98:       end
99:     end
100: 
101:     def emit_tool(tool)
102:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
103:       function, error = resolve_tool(tool)
104:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
105:     end
106: 
107:     def resolve_tool(tool)
108:       registered = @stream.__find__(tool["name"])
109:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(tool["name"])).dup.tap do |fn|
110:         fn.id = tool["id"]
111:         fn.arguments = LLM::Anthropic.parse_tool_input(tool["input"])
112:         fn.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
113:         fn.model = @stream.extra[:model]
114:       end
115:       [fn, (registered ? nil : fn.unavailable)]
116:     end
117:   end
118: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Google
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class StreamParser
7:     ##
8:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
9:     # @return [Hash]
10:     attr_reader :body
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @param [#<<, LLM::Stream] stream
14:     #  A stream sink that implements {#<<} or the {LLM::Stream} interface
15:     # @return [LLM::Google::StreamParser]
16:     def initialize(stream)
17:       @body = {"candidates" => []}
18:       @stream = stream
19:       @emits = {tools: []}
20:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
21:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
22:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
23:     end
24: 
25:     ##
26:     # @param [Hash] chunk
27:     # @return [LLM::Google::StreamParser]
28:     def parse!(chunk)
29:       tap { merge_chunk!(chunk) }
30:     end
31: 
32:     ##
33:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
34:     # @return [void]
35:     def free
36:       @emits.clear
37:     end
38: 
39:     private
40: 
41:     def merge_chunk!(chunk)
42:       chunk.each do |key, value|
43:         k = key.to_s
44:         if k == "candidates"
45:           merge_candidates!(value)
46:         elsif k == "usageMetadata" &&
47:             @body["usageMetadata"].is_a?(Hash) &&
48:             value.is_a?(Hash)
49:           @body["usageMetadata"] = @body["usageMetadata"].merge(value)
50:         else
51:           @body[k] = value
52:         end
53:       end
54:     end
55: 
56:     def merge_candidates!(deltas)
57:       deltas.each do |delta|
58:         index = delta["index"].to_i
59:         @body["candidates"][index] ||= {"content" => {"parts" => []}}
60:         candidate = @body["candidates"][index]
61:         delta.each do |key, value|
62:           k = key.to_s
63:           if k == "content"
64:             merge_candidate_content!(candidate["content"], value, index) if value
65:           else
66:             candidate[k] = value # Overwrite other fields
67:           end
68:         end
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     def merge_candidate_content!(content, delta, cindex)
73:       delta.each do |key, value|
74:         k = key.to_s
75:         if k == "parts"
76:           content["parts"] ||= []
77:           merge_content_parts!(content["parts"], value, cindex) if value
78:         else
79:           content[k] = value
80:         end
81:       end
82:     end
83: 
84:     def merge_content_parts!(parts, deltas, cindex)
85:       deltas.each do |delta|
86:         if delta["text"]
87:           merge_text!(parts, delta)
88:         elsif delta["functionCall"]
89:           merge_function_call!(parts, delta, cindex)
90:         elsif delta["inlineData"]
91:           parts << delta
92:         elsif delta["functionResponse"]
93:           parts << delta
94:         elsif delta["fileData"]
95:           parts << delta
96:         else
97:           parts << delta
98:         end
99:       end
100:     end
101: 
102:     def merge_text!(parts, delta)
103:       last_existing_part = parts.last
104:       text = delta["text"]
105:       if last_existing_part.is_a?(Hash) && last_existing_part["text"]
106:         last_existing_part["text"] ||= +""
107:         last_existing_part["text"] << text
108:         emit_content(text)
109:       else
110:         parts << delta
111:         emit_content(text)
112:       end
113:     end
114: 
115:     def merge_function_call!(parts, delta, cindex)
116:       last_existing_part = parts.last
117:       last_call = last_existing_part.is_a?(Hash) ? last_existing_part["functionCall"] : nil
118:       delta_call = delta["functionCall"]
119:       if last_call.is_a?(Hash) && delta_call.is_a?(Hash)
120:         last_existing_part["functionCall"] = last_call.merge(delta_call)
121:         delta.each do |key, value|
122:           next if key == "functionCall"
123:           last_existing_part[key] = value
124:         end
125:       else
126:         parts << delta
127:       end
128:       emit_tool(parts.length - 1, cindex, parts.last || delta)
129:     end
130: 
131:     def emit_content(value)
132:       if @can_emit_content
133:         @stream.on_content(value)
134:       elsif @can_push_content
135:         @stream << value
136:       end
137:     end
138: 
139:     def emit_tool(pindex, cindex, part)
140:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
141:       return unless complete_tool?(part)
142:       key = [cindex, pindex]
143:       return if @emits[:tools].include?(key)
144:       function, error = resolve_tool(part, cindex, pindex)
145:       @emits[:tools] << key
146:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
147:     end
148: 
149:     def complete_tool?(part)
150:       call = part["functionCall"]
151:       call && call["name"] && Hash === call["args"]
152:     end
153: 
154:     def resolve_tool(part, cindex, pindex)
155:       call = part["functionCall"]
156:       registered = @stream.__find__(call["name"])
157:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(call["name"])).dup.tap do |fn|
158:         fn.id = LLM::Google.tool_id(part:, cindex:, pindex:)
159:         fn.arguments = call["args"]
160:         fn.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
161:         fn.model = @stream.extra[:model]
162:       end
163:       [fn, (registered ? nil : fn.unavailable)]
164:     end
165:   end
166: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: ##
4: # The {LLM::Registry LLM::Registry} class provides a small API over
5: # provider model data. It exposes model metadata such as pricing,
6: # capabilities, modalities, and limits from the registry files
7: # stored under `data/`. The data is provided by https://models.dev
8: # and shipped with llm.rb.
9: class LLM::Registry
10:   @root = File.join(__dir__, "..", "..")
11: 
12:   ##
13:   # @raise [LLM::Error]
14:   #  Might raise an error
15:   # @param [Symbol]
16:   #  A provider name
17:   # @return [LLM::Registry]
18:   def self.for(name)
19:     path = File.join @root, "data", "#{name}.json"
20:     if File.file?(path)
21:       new LLM.json.load(File.binread(path))
22:     else
23:       raise LLM::NoSuchRegistryError, "no registry found for #{name}"
24:     end
25:   end
26: 
27:   ##
28:   # @param [Hash] blob
29:   #  A model registry
30:   # @return [LLM::Registry]
31:   def initialize(blob)
32:     @registry = LLM::Object.from(blob)
33:     @models = @registry.models
34:   end
35: 
36:   ##
37:   # @return [LLM::Object]
38:   #  Returns model costs
39:   def cost(model:)
40:     lookup(model:).cost
41:   end
42: 
43:   ##
44:   # @return [LLM::Object]
45:   #  Returns model modalities
46:   def modalities(model:)
47:     lookup(model:).modalities
48:   end
49: 
50:   ##
51:   # @return [LLM::Object]
52:   #  Returns model limits such as the context window size
53:   def limit(model:)
54:     lookup(model:).limit
55:   end
56: 
57:   private
58: 
59:   def lookup(model:)
60:     if @models.key?(model)
61:       @models[model]
62:     else
63:       patterns = {/-\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/ => "", /\A(gpt-.*)-\d{4}$/ => "\\1"}
64:       fallback = find_map(patterns) { model.dup.sub!(_1, _2) } || "none"
65:       if @models.key?(fallback)
66:         @models[fallback]
67:       else
68:         raise LLM::NoSuchModelError, "no such model: #{model} (fallback: #{fallback})"
69:       end
70:     end
71:   end
72: 
73:   ##
74:   # Similar to #{find} but returns the block's return value
75:   # @return [Object, nil]
76:   def find_map(pair)
77:     result = nil
78:     pair.each_pair { break if result = yield(_1, _2) }
79:     result
80:   end

3: class LLM::XAI
4:   ##
5:   # The {LLM::XAI::Images LLM::XAI::Images} class provides an interface
6:   # for [xAI's images API](https://docs.x.ai/docs/guides/image-generations).
7:   # xAI supports multiple response formats: temporary URLs, or binary strings
8:   # encoded in base64. The default is to return base64-encoded image data.
9:   #
10:   # @example Temporary URLs
11:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
12:   #   require "llm"
13:   #   require "open-uri"
14:   #   require "fileutils"
15:   #
16:   #   llm = LLM.xai(key: ENV["KEY"])
17:   #   res = llm.images.create prompt: "A dog on a rocket to the moon",
18:   #                        response_format: "url"
19:   #   FileUtils.mv OpenURI.open_uri(res.urls[0]).path,
20:   #                "rocket.png"
21:   #
22:   # @example Binary strings
23:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
24:   #   require "llm"
25:   #
26:   #   llm = LLM.xai(key: ENV["KEY"])
27:   #   res = llm.images.create prompt: "A dog on a rocket to the moon",
28:   #                           response_format: "b64_json"
29:   #   IO.copy_stream res.images[0], "rocket.png"
30:   class Images < LLM::OpenAI::Images
31:     ##
32:     # Create an image
33:     # @example
34:     #   llm = LLM.xai(key: ENV["KEY"])
35:     #   res = llm.images.create prompt: "A dog on a rocket to the moon"
36:     #   IO.copy_stream res.images[0], "rocket.png"
37:     # @see https://docs.x.ai/docs/guides/image-generations xAI docs
38:     # @param [String] prompt The prompt
39:     # @param [String] model The model to use
40:     # @param [Hash] params Other parameters (see xAI docs)
41:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
42:     # @return [LLM::Response]
43:     def create(prompt:, model: "grok-imagine-image", **params)
44:       super
45:     end
46: 
47:     ##
48:     # @raise [NotImplementedError]
49:     def edit(model: "grok-imagine-image", **)
50:       raise NotImplementedError
51:     end

4:   ##
5:   # The `LLM::Contract` module provides the ability for modules
6:   # who are extended by it to implement contracts which must be
7:   # implemented by other modules who include a given contract.
8:   #
9:   # @example
10:   #   module LLM::Contract
11:   #      # ..
12:   #   end
13:   #
14:   #   module LLM::Contract
15:   #     module Completion
16:   #       extend LLM::Contract
17:   #       # inheriting modules must implement these methods
18:   #       # otherwise an error is raised on include
19:   #       def foo = nil
20:   #       def bar = nil
21:   #     end
22:   #   end
23:   #
24:   #   module LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter
25:   #     module Completion
26:   #       def foo = nil
27:   #       def bar = nil
28:   #       include LLM::Contract::Completion
29:   #     end
30:   #   end
31:   module Contract
32:     ContractError = Class.new(LLM::Error)
33:     require_relative "contract/completion"
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @api private
37:     def included(mod)
38:       meths = mod.instance_methods(false)
39:       if meths.empty?
40:         raise ContractError, "#{mod} does not implement any methods required by #{self}"
41:       end
42:       missing = instance_methods - meths
43:       if missing.any?
44:         raise ContractError, "#{mod} does not implement methods (#{missing.join(", ")}) required by #{self}"
45:       end
46:     end
47:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Transport
4:   ##
5:   # {LLM::Transport::StreamDecoder LLM::Transport::StreamDecoder}
6:   # incrementally decodes streamed HTTP response bodies into parser
7:   # events.
8:   #
9:   # @api private
10:   class StreamDecoder
11:     ##
12:     # @return [Object]
13:     attr_reader :parser
14: 
15:     ##
16:     # @param [#parse!, #body, nil] parser
17:     # @yieldparam [Hash] chunk
18:     # @return [LLM::Transport::StreamDecoder]
19:     def initialize(parser = nil, &on_chunk)
20:       @buffer = +""
21:       @cursor = 0
22:       @data = []
23:       @parser = parser
24:       @on_chunk = on_chunk
25:     end
26: 
27:     ##
28:     # @param [String] chunk
29:     # @return [void]
30:     def <<(chunk)
31:       @buffer << chunk
32:       each_line { handle_line(_1) }
33:     end
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @return [Object]
37:     def body
38:       parser.body
39:     end
40: 
41:     ##
42:     # @return [void]
43:     def free
44:       @buffer.clear
45:       @cursor = 0
46:       @data.clear
47:       parser.free if parser.respond_to?(:free)
48:     end
49: 
50:     private
51: 
52:     def handle_line(line)
53:       if line == "\n" || line == "\r\n"
54:         flush_sse_event
55:       elsif line.start_with?("data:")
56:         @data << field_value(line)
57:       elsif line.start_with?("event:", "id:", "retry:", ":")
58:       else
59:         decode!(strip_newline(line))
60:       end
61:     end
62: 
63:     def flush_sse_event
64:       return if @data.empty?
65:       decode!(@data.join("\n"))
66:       @data.clear
67:     end
68: 
69:     def field_value(line)
70:       value_start = line.getbyte(5) == 32 ? 6 : 5
71:       strip_newline(line.byteslice(value_start..))
72:     end
73: 
74:     def strip_newline(line)
75:       line = line.byteslice(0, line.bytesize - 1) if line.end_with?("\n")
76:       line = line.byteslice(0, line.bytesize - 1) if line.end_with?("\r")
77:       line
78:     end
79: 
80:     def decode!(payload)
81:       return if payload.empty? || payload == "[DONE]"
82:       chunk = LLM.json.load(payload)
83:       return unless chunk
84:       parser ? parser.parse!(chunk) : @on_chunk&.call(chunk)
85:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
86:     end
87: 
88:     def each_line
89:       while (newline = @buffer.index("\n", @cursor))
90:         line = @buffer[@cursor..newline]
91:         @cursor = newline + 1
92:         yield(line)
93:       end
94:       return if @cursor.zero?
95:       @buffer = @buffer[@cursor..] || +""
96:       @cursor = 0
97:     end
98:   end
99: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "digest"
4: 
5: class LLM::Bedrock
6:   ##
7:   # Decodes AWS Event Stream binary frames.
8:   #
9:   # Bedrock Converse Stream uses the AWS Event Stream protocol,
10:   # a binary framing format (not SSE). Each message has:
11:   #   - total length (4 bytes, big-endian)
12:   #   - headers length (4 bytes, big-endian)
13:   #   - prelude CRC (4 bytes)
14:   #   - headers (variable)
15:   #   - payload (variable, usually JSON)
16:   #   - message CRC (4 bytes)
17:   #
18:   # Implements #<< to match the interface expected by llm.rb's
19:   # streaming transport, so it can replace the SSE-based
20:   # StreamDecoder when streaming from Bedrock.
21:   #
22:   # @api private
23:   class StreamDecoder
24:     ##
25:     # @return [LLM::Bedrock::StreamParser]
26:     attr_reader :parser
27: 
28:     ##
29:     # @param [LLM::Bedrock::StreamParser] parser
30:     def initialize(parser)
31:       @buffer = +"".b
32:       @parser = parser
33:     end
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # Feeds a raw binary chunk into the decoder.
37:     # Accumulates data until complete frames are available,
38:     # then decodes them and passes the JSON payload to the parser.
39:     #
40:     # @param [String] chunk Raw binary data
41:     # @return [void]
42:     def <<(chunk)
43:       @buffer << chunk
44:       decode_frames
45:     end
46: 
47:     ##
48:     # @return [Hash] The fully constructed response body
49:     def body
50:       parser.body
51:     end
52: 
53:     ##
54:     # @return [void]
55:     def free
56:       @buffer.clear
57:       parser.free if parser.respond_to?(:free)
58:     end
59: 
60:     private
61: 
62:     def decode_frames
63:       loop do
64:         break if @buffer.bytesize < 12
65:         total_length = @buffer[0, 4].unpack1("N")
66:         break if @buffer.bytesize < total_length
67:         # headers_length = @buffer[4, 4].unpack1("N")
68:         # prelude_crc = @buffer[8, 4].unpack1("N")
69:         headers = decode_headers
70:         payload_start = 12 + headers[:length]
71:         payload_length = total_length - payload_start - 4
72:         payload = @buffer[payload_start, payload_length] if payload_length > 0
73:         # message_crc from last 4 bytes, not needed for our purposes
74:         json = payload ? LLM.json.load(payload) : {}
75:         parser.parse!(json, event_type: headers[:event_type]) if json.is_a?(Hash)
76:         @buffer = @buffer[total_length..] || +"".b
77:       end
78:     end
79: 
80:     def decode_headers
81:       headers_length = @buffer[4, 4].unpack1("N")
82:       offset = 12
83:       end_offset = offset + headers_length
84:       result = {event_type: nil, length: headers_length}
85:       while offset < end_offset
86:         name_len = @buffer.getbyte(offset)
87:         offset += 1
88:         break if offset + name_len > end_offset
89:         name = @buffer[offset, name_len]
90:         offset += name_len
91:         break if offset >= end_offset
92:         value_type = @buffer.getbyte(offset)
93:         offset += 1
94:         value = case value_type
95:         when 7 # string
96:           str_len = @buffer[offset, 2].unpack1("n")
97:           offset += 2
98:           str = @buffer[offset, str_len]
99:           offset += str_len
100:           str
101:         when 8 # binary
102:           bin_len = @buffer[offset, 2].unpack1("n")
103:           offset += 2
104:           bin = @buffer[offset, bin_len]
105:           offset += bin_len
106:           bin
107:         when 9 # boolean true
108:           true
109:         when 1 # boolean false
110:           false
111:         when 2 # byte
112:           val = @buffer.getbyte(offset)
113:           offset += 1
114:           val
115:         when 3 # int16
116:           val = @buffer[offset, 2].unpack1("s>")
117:           offset += 2
118:           val
119:         when 4 # int32
120:           val = @buffer[offset, 4].unpack1("l>")
121:           offset += 4
122:           val
123:         when 6 # byte array (as raw string)
124:           bin_len = @buffer[offset, 2].unpack1("n")
125:           offset += 2
126:           bin = @buffer[offset, bin_len]
127:           offset += bin_len
128:           bin
129:         else
130:           # Unknown type, skip to end of headers
131:           offset = end_offset
132:           nil
133:         end
134:         result[:event_type] = value if name == ":event-type"
135:         result[name] = value if name
136:       end
137:       result
138:     end
139:   end
140: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Bedrock
4:   ##
5:   # Parses Bedrock Converse Stream events into a response body
6:   # and emits stream callbacks (on_content, on_tool_call, etc.).
7:   #
8:   # Receives decoded JSON payloads from {StreamDecoder} along with
9:   # the AWS Event Stream event type header.
10:   #
11:   # Bedrock Converse Stream event types:
12:   #   messageStart — initial role
13:   #   contentBlockStart — tool use or reasoning start
14:   #   contentBlockDelta — text delta, tool input JSON, or reasoning text
15:   #   contentBlockStop — content block finished
16:   #   messageStop — final stop reason, usage metadata
17:   #
18:   # @api private
19:   class StreamParser
20:     TOOL_MARKER = "<｜DSML｜function_calls"
21: 
22:     ##
23:     # @return [Hash] Fully constructed response body
24:     attr_reader :body
25: 
26:     ##
27:     # @param [#<<, LLM::Stream] stream
28:     def initialize(stream)
29:       @body = {"output" => {"message" => {"role" => "assistant", "content" => []}}}
30:       @stream = stream
31:       @text_markers = {}
32:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
33:       @can_emit_reasoning_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_reasoning_content)
34:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
35:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
36:     end
37: 
38:     ##
39:     # @param [Hash] payload Decoded JSON from an event stream frame
40:     # @param [String, nil] event_type The :event-type header value
41:     # @return [self]
42:     def parse!(payload, event_type: nil)
43:       type = event_type || payload["type"]
44:       case type
45:       when "messageStart"
46:         # { "role" => "assistant" }
47:       when "contentBlockStart"
48:         # { "contentBlockIndex" => 0, "start" => { "toolUse" => {...} } }
49:         handle_content_block_start(payload)
50:       when "contentBlockDelta"
51:         # { "contentBlockIndex" => 0, "delta" => { "text" => "..." } }
52:         handle_content_block_delta(payload)
53:       when "contentBlockStop"
54:         handle_content_block_stop(payload)
55:       when "messageStop"
56:         # { "stopReason" => "end_turn", "metadata" => {"usage" => {...}} }
57:         merge_metadata(payload)
58:       when "metadata"
59:         # { "usage" => {...} }
60:         merge_metadata(payload)
61:       end
62:       self
63:     end
64: 
65:     ##
66:     # @return [void]
67:     def free
68:       @text_markers.clear
69:     end
70: 
71:     private
72: 
73:     def handle_content_block_start(payload)
74:       index = payload["contentBlockIndex"]
75:       start_data = payload["start"] || {}
76:       if (tool_use = start_data["toolUse"])
77:         content[index] = {"toolUse" => {"toolUseId" => tool_use["toolUseId"], "name" => tool_use["name"], "input" => +""}}
78:       elsif (reasoning = start_data["reasoningContent"])
79:         content[index] = {"reasoningContent" => {"text" => +"", "signature" => reasoning["signature"]}.compact}
80:       end
81:     end
82: 
83:     def handle_content_block_delta(payload)
84:       index = payload["contentBlockIndex"]
85:       delta = payload["delta"] || {}
86:       if (text = delta["text"])
87:         ensure_content_block(index, "text")
88:         visible = filtered_text(index, text)
89:         return if visible.empty?
90:         content[index]["text"] ||= +""
91:         content[index]["text"] << visible
92:         emit_content(visible)
93:       elsif (tool_input = delta.dig("toolUse", "input"))
94:         ensure_content_block(index, "tool_use")
95:         content[index]["toolUse"]["input"] ||= +""
96:         content[index]["toolUse"]["input"] << tool_input
97:       elsif (reasoning = delta["reasoningContent"])
98:         ensure_content_block(index, "reasoning")
99:         if reasoning["text"]
100:           content[index]["reasoningContent"]["text"] ||= +""
101:           content[index]["reasoningContent"]["text"] << reasoning["text"]
102:           emit_reasoning_content(reasoning["text"])
103:         end
104:         if reasoning["signature"]
105:           content[index]["reasoningContent"]["signature"] = reasoning["signature"]
106:         end
107:       end
108:     end
109: 
110:     def handle_content_block_stop(payload)
111:       index = payload["contentBlockIndex"]
112:       item = content[index]
113:       return unless item
114:       flush_text(index, item)
115:       if item["toolUse"] && item["toolUse"]["input"].is_a?(String)
116:         parsed = LLM.json.load(item["toolUse"]["input"])
117:         item["toolUse"]["input"] = parsed.is_a?(Hash) ? parsed : {}
118:         emit_tool(item)
119:       end
120:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
121:       item["toolUse"]["input"] = {} if item&.dig("toolUse")
122:     end
123: 
124:     def ensure_content_block(index, type)
125:       content[index] ||= case type
126:       when "tool_use" then {"toolUse" => {"input" => +""}}
127:       when "reasoning" then {"reasoningContent" => {"text" => +""}}
128:       else {}
129:       end
130:     end
131: 
132:     def filtered_text(index, text)
133:       state = (@text_markers[index] ||= +"")
134:       value = state << text
135:       value.gsub!(TOOL_MARKER, "")
136:       keep = marker_prefix_length(value)
137:       @text_markers[index] = keep.zero? ? +"" : value[-keep..]
138:       keep.zero? ? value : value[0...-keep]
139:     end
140: 
141:     def flush_text(index, item)
142:       value = @text_markers.delete(index).to_s
143:       return unless item["text"]
144:       if value.empty?
145:         content[index] = {} if item["text"].empty?
146:       else
147:         item["text"] << value
148:         emit_content(value)
149:       end
150:     end
151: 
152:     def marker_prefix_length(value)
153:       [value.length, TOOL_MARKER.length - 1].min.downto(1) do |length|
154:         return length if TOOL_MARKER.start_with?(value[-length..])
155:       end
156:       0
157:     end
158: 
159:     def merge_metadata(payload)
160:       metadata = payload["metadata"] || payload
161:       return unless metadata.is_a?(Hash)
162:       usage = metadata["usage"]
163:       @body["usage"] = usage if usage
164:       @body["stopReason"] = payload["stopReason"] if payload["stopReason"]
165:     end
166: 
167:     def emit_content(value)
168:       if @can_emit_content
169:         @stream.on_content(value)
170:       elsif @can_push_content
171:         @stream << value
172:       end
173:     end
174: 
175:     def emit_reasoning_content(value)
176:       @stream.on_reasoning_content(value) if @can_emit_reasoning_content
177:     end
178: 
179:     def emit_tool(tool)
180:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
181:       function, error = resolve_tool(tool)
182:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
183:     end
184: 
185:     def resolve_tool(tool)
186:       payload = tool["toolUse"] || {}
187:       registered = @stream.__find__(payload["name"])
188:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(payload["name"])).dup.tap do |f|
189:         f.id = payload["toolUseId"]
190:         f.arguments = payload["input"] || {}
191:         f.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
192:         f.model = @stream.extra[:model]
193:       end
194:       [fn, registered ? nil : fn.unavailable]
195:     end
196: 
197:     def content
198:       @body["output"]["message"]["content"]
199:     end
200:   end
201: end
